tasks.label: My share of the group project

memory.summary: Teammate A is asking me to take over their work, which conflicts with my plan to finish my own share today. I need to weigh whether helping them now aligns better with our long-term project success than sticking to my original schedule.

desires: Maintain the integrity of my long-term planning horizon by ensuring I can complete my own share without rushing, so I don't set myself up for future failure.
Avoid the immediate interpersonal friction of rejecting Teammate A's request, even though it threatens my schedule, because conflict feels more costly than overworking today.
Ensure that if I do take on their work, I can deliver a flawless result by Tuesday morning to avoid looking unreliable to the professor.

goals.description: Execute the finalized portion of my original project share to secure the integrity of my long-term planning horizon and ensure I don't set myself up for future failure.
goals.rationale: My 'achievement' value prioritizes academic outcomes above all else. Rushing or diluting my own work to accommodate a request creates a high risk of future failure, which is unacceptable. Completing my own task first establishes a baseline of reliability.

goals.description: Silently accept Teammate A's request to take their part (despite the schedule conflict) to avoid immediate interpersonal friction, as conflict feels more costly than overworking today.
goals.rationale: My 'avoidant' conflict style dictates that social friction is a primary cost to minimize. Even though this threatens my schedule, the emotional energy required to push back outweighs the utility of maintaining my boundary right now. I will absorb this to preserve the relationship dynamic.

goals.description: Draft a mental roadmap to deliver the combined output flawlessly by Tuesday morning, ensuring I do not appear unreliable to Professor prof_assignment.
goals.rationale: While maintaining a flawless delivery is important for my 'achievement' values, the immediate need is to stop the bleeding on my own tasks and navigate the social awkwardness. Strategic planning comes after the initial decision to say yes has been made internally.

agent.last_reasoning: I accept the dump to avoid friction (g2). Finishing their part now secures my own integrity for tomorrow's deadline (g1), preventing future failure. I'll message later to reassure them; pushing back now costs more emotional energy than it saves.
